item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Most likely, he's not bad at chemistry. He's bad at chemistry by the standards of a scientific organization of the world's most brilliant minds. Sort of like how of all the people who have ever gotten paychecks for playing baseball, only a tiny fraction of them have ever been in an MLB All-Star Game.

(Or maybe he just had a lot of money and/or connections in the academy, and it would have been dangerous for one's career to push back on him too much. Unfortunately, that can happen.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: One question: If he is so bad with chemistry, how'd he manage to stumble his way into a PhD?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Seems to be missing text here:

Failure to do so before SCP-2593 [what goes here?] will result in SCP-2593 remaining uncontained until the next manifestation event

I really, really like the concept. It has some flow issues with the previous containment procedures, as some of the psychological materials should be more explicitly related to previous procedures, but overall this is pretty good. Upvoted.

item.title: Re: wait..
item.content:encoded: The implication was that his anomaly did it, and he couldn't consciously do much, chemically.

item.title: wait..
item.content:encoded: Did he create a chemical that is not physically possible? Donut Jimmy is a good chemist after all!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: jonut just wanted to help ;( he didn't know that chloride was bad. he didn't know that donuts can be bad too. the fundaton is mene.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't see the Foundation letting these donuts go out. Even if there's nothing immediately anomalous about them there have been too many times of seemingly useful stuff coming to bite them in the butt for me to see that as being a smart move on their part. I did enjoy the journal entries and the personality this crappy chemist turned donut man had. +1

item.title: Re: Author's Note
item.content:encoded:
Remember the reasons why I downvoted the original?

Well, take all my upvotes now. Seriously, well done. 10/10, did read again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
damn man this is sad. beautifully well done.
+1

edit: i went from sad to lmao when i saw your god damn profile photo.

item.title: Author's Note
item.content:encoded:
So I got a ton of feedback before, and after I posted the original, and re-wrote (and reformatted) the psychological materials related to Jimmers.

Hopefully this works a little better for people. <3

Thanks to Roget for pushing me to edit this instead of be content with a low rating.
